title: Centre for Advanced Manufacturing and Supply Chains - Home
gist: Promotional WEF landing page for its Centre for Advanced Manufacturing and Supply Chains; mostly initiative descriptions with a few concrete figures (223 Global Lighthouse Network factories, 222 business partners) and the launch of Lumina, an AI intelligence platform for industrial transformation.

insights.claim: The World Economic Forum's Global Lighthouse Network has grown to 223 factories implementing Fourth Industrial Revolution technologies to boost productivity, sustainability and supply-chain resilience.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: Tracks the breadth of advanced-manufacturing tech adoption; a benchmark cohort for identifying which manufacturers are operationalizing 4IR/AI tooling.
insights.quote: 223 factories are now engaged in the network, implementing advanced manufacturing technologies and boosting productivity, sustainability and resilient supply chains.
insights.locator: Global Lighthouse Network: Transforming advanced manufacturing

insights.claim: WEF has launched Lumina, an AI-enabled intelligence platform built on eight years of Global Lighthouse Network data to turn audited evidence and peer experience into decision intelligence for industrial transformation.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: Indicates AI-driven decision-intelligence tooling is moving into industrial operations; a theme for industrial-software and automation exposure.
insights.quote: Lumina is an AI-enabled intelligence platform for industrial transformation. Built on eight years of the Global Lighthouse Network (GLN)
